title: [CI P1] Add impact-aware parallel frontend validation
description:
## Summary

Split the unconditional serial frontend job into impact-aware static and real-browser jobs that run in parallel, while preserving `test-frontend` as the stable always-running required-check context.

Coordinate with #3324 and #3326 because this issue extends the shared selector and should reuse the same changed-file and pure-aggregator patterns.

## Problem

The current `test-frontend` job runs for every pull request, push, manual run, and scheduled run. It sequentially performs:

1. repository checkout;
2. Node setup and `npm ci`;
3. TypeScript checking;
4. ESLint;
5. Vitest unit/component tests;
6. a production Vite build and manifest verification;
7. Playwright installation with Chromium, Firefox, and OS dependencies;
8. real-browser regression tests;
9. upload of `dashboard-dist`.

Known backend-only changes therefore pay the full frontend cost. Ordinary frontend changes also wait for static validation and both browser engines serially. The browser binaries and system packages are installed on every run, and the uploaded `dashboard-dist` artifact has no current workflow consumer.

## Target graph

```text
select-test-suites
       ├── frontend-static
       └── frontend-browser (Chromium, optionally Firefox)
                         \       /
                          test-frontend
```

`frontend-static` and browser validation should run in parallel. `test-frontend` should become a tiny always-running result aggregator with no checkout, preserving the existing required-check identity.

## Selector contract

Add outputs:

```text
frontend_static=true|false
frontend_browser_chromium=true|false
frontend_browser_firefox=true|false
full_frontend=true|false
```

Enforce these invariants:

```text
frontend_browser_firefox => frontend_browser_chromium
frontend_browser_chromium => frontend_static
full_frontend => all frontend outputs
```

### Recommended selection policy

| Change category | Static | Chromium | Firefox |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Known backend-only change | No | No | No |
| Generated OpenAPI client/generator change | Yes | No | No |
| TypeScript, React, template, or UI source change | Yes | Yes | No |
| CSS/layout/browser-test/browser-config change | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| `package.json` or `package-lock.json` | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Push to `main` | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Scheduled or manual full run | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Unknown path or unavailable change set | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Documentation-only change | No | No | No |

### Suggested path ownership

Static-impact paths should include at least:

```text
frontend/**
api_service/templates/**
package.json
package-lock.json
postcss.config.cjs
tailwind.config.cjs
frontend/vite.config.ts
frontend/tsconfig.json
frontend/eslint.config.mjs
frontend/src/generated/openapi.ts
tools/test_unit.sh
tools/run_repo_python.sh
tools/verify_vite_manifest.py
tools/export_openapi.py
tools/generate_openapi_types.py
```

Chromium-impact paths should include UI source, templates, and real-browser test inputs.

Firefox-on-PR paths should include at least:

```text
frontend/src/browser/**
frontend/src/styles/**
frontend/vitest.browser.config.ts
package.json
package-lock.json
```

A generated OpenAPI file change should select static TypeScript validation but should not require real-browser testing by itself.

## Implementation

### 1. Add `frontend-static`

The job should:

- depend on selector output;
- use shallow checkout;
- use Node's npm cache;
- run `npm ci --no-audit --no-fund`;
- run the existing canonical static command:

```bash
npm run frontend:ci
```

The current script already includes typecheck, lint, unit/component tests, build, and Vite-manifest verification.

### 2. Add a browser-engine matrix

Create a conditional `frontend-browser` matrix:

- ordinary UI-impacting PRs: Chromium only;
- browser-sensitive PRs, `main`, scheduled, and manual full runs: Chromium and Firefox;
- `fail-fast: false` so one engine still reports when the other fails;
- one engine per matrix leg for clear timing and failure attribution.

### 3. Parameterize the Vitest browser configuration

`frontend/vitest.browser.config.ts` currently hardcodes Chromium and Firefox. Add an environment-driven engine selector, for example:

```text
MOONMIND_BROWSER_ENGINES=chromium
MOONMIND_BROWSER_ENGINES=firefox
MOONMIND_BROWSER_ENGINES=chromium,firefox
```

Keep the local default as both engines so `npm run ui:test:browser` preserves existing developer behavior.

### 4. Use a pinned Playwright environment

Prefer a pinned Playwright container with an immutable digest rather than running:

```bash
npx playwright install --with-deps chromium firefox
```

Requirements:

- add `playwright` as an explicit, exact dev dependency if it is currently only transitive;
- pin the container to the matching Playwright version and image digest;
- add a workflow contract test that verifies the package-lock version and workflow image version stay aligned;
- run `npx playwright --version` as a cheap environment diagnostic;
- do not download browser engines and install OS packages on every run.

If a container is not viable, cache `~/.cache/ms-playwright` by exact locked Playwright version and engine, and keep system dependency installation separately controlled.

### 5. Preserve `test-frontend` as an always-running aggregator

Reuse the current `test-frontend` job ID. It should:

- have `if: always()`;
- depend on selector, static, and browser jobs;
- contain no checkout, Node setup, or repository command;
- pass with an explicit "intentionally skipped" summary for known backend-only changes;
- fail when a selected frontend job is failed, cancelled, timed out, or unexpectedly skipped;
- summarize engine-level outcomes in `GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY`;
- preserve the required-check context `test-frontend`.

### 6. Remove the unused `dashboard-dist` upload

Remove the artifact upload in this issue. Do not immediately make Docker publishing consume it.

The Dockerfile currently performs its own reproducible frontend build. Cross-workflow artifact reuse requires exact SHA/run provenance, manifest and hash verification, and architecture-build coordination. That is outside this issue and can be revisited after the CD workflow is explicitly gated on required CI.

### 7. Keep generated-contract verification separate

Do not fold `check-generated-contracts` into `frontend-static`. Preserve its stable conditional detector and always-running result job. Frontend selection may react to changes in the generated client, but OpenAPI source-impact detection remains independently required.

## Tests

### Selector tests

Add cases for:

- backend-only change skips frontend;
- frontend source selects static plus Chromium;
- generated OpenAPI client selects static only;
- browser fixture selects both engines;
- browser config selects both engines;
- package-lock change selects full frontend;
- docs-only change skips frontend;
- `main`, schedule, manual, and unknown-path cases select full frontend;
- Firefox selection implies Chromium and static.

Refactor existing selector tests that assert every output is true, because backend and frontend fullness are now separate concepts.

### Workflow contract tests

Prove that:

- `frontend-static` and `frontend-browser` are selector-controlled;
- browser validation uses a one- or two-engine matrix;
- no browser-install-with-deps step remains;
- package and container Playwright versions match;
- `test-frontend` is always-running and contains no checkout;
- selected failures propagate to `test-frontend`;
- unselected frontend checks produce an intentional pass;
- no `dashboard-dist` artifact is uploaded.

## Expected files

- `tools/select_test_suites.py`
- `.github/workflows/pytest-unit-tests.yml`
- `frontend/vitest.browser.config.ts`
- `package.json`
- `package-lock.json`
- `tests/unit/tools/test_select_test_suites.py`
- `tests/unit/test_pytest_unit_workflow.py` or a dedicated frontend workflow contract test
- `docs/Development/BackendTestSelection.md`
- `docs/Development/PreCommitWorkflow.md`
- `docs/Development/FrontendDevelopment.md`
- `docs/UI/TypeScriptSystem.md`

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Known backend-only PRs do not start static or browser frontend runners.
- [ ] Ordinary frontend PRs run static validation and Chromium in parallel.
- [ ] Firefox runs on `main`, scheduled/manual full runs, and browser-sensitive PRs.
- [ ] `test-frontend` remains the stable always-running required-check context.
- [ ] Local `npm run ui:test:browser` still runs both engines by default.
- [ ] Browser binaries and OS dependencies are supplied by a pinned environment rather than installed on every run.
- [ ] The `dashboard-dist` artifact upload is removed.
- [ ] Generated-contract verification remains a separate stable required result.
- [ ] Selector and workflow tests cover every frontend output and fail-open condition.

## Non-goals

- Making Docker publishing consume the frontend artifact.
- Removing Docker's duplicate frontend checks before exact-commit CI gating exists.
- Replacing Vitest or Playwright.
- Reducing browser coverage below Chromium for ordinary UI PRs or below Chromium plus Firefox for full/browser-sensitive runs.
acceptance_criteria: Known backend-only PRs do not start static or browser frontend runners.
Ordinary frontend PRs run static validation and Chromium in parallel.
Firefox runs on `main`, scheduled/manual full runs, and browser-sensitive PRs.
`test-frontend` remains the stable always-running required-check context.
Local `npm run ui:test:browser` still runs both engines by default.
Browser binaries and OS dependencies are supplied by a pinned environment rather than installed on every run.
Generated-contract verification remains a separate stable required result.
Selector and workflow tests cover every frontend output and fail-open condition.
